source: Tier-1 deterministic (endpoint smoke + code read) — independent of agent fleet
method: exercised live :8099 endpoints + traced root cause in source (evidence-first, reproduced)

findings.name: ollamaHost / listModels / resolveLocalCoder / resolveDefaultModel / aiGenerate
findings.symptom: ollamaHost() returns process.env.OLLAMA_HOST (set to the docker value host.docker.internal:11434 in .env). On any local/non-docker boot that host is unreachable, so listModels() fetch(`${ollamaHost()}/api/tags`) (ai.ts:42) throws 'fetch failed' -> GET /api/ai/models returns 500, and resolveLocalCoder/resolveDefaultModel/aiGenerate (the whole colab-mirror /api/ai/* surface) break. server/providers.ts:409-411 already has the [OLLAMA_HOST, localhost, 127.0.0.1] reachability fallback; ai.ts does NOT mirror it.
findings.evidence: GET http://127.0.0.1:8099/api/ai/models -> 500 {"error":"fetch failed"} (live, reproduced). Code: ai.ts:21 ollamaHost def; ai.ts:42 unguarded fetch; providers.ts:409-411 the fallback it should mirror.
findings.fix: Extract a shared resolveOllamaBase() that tries [process.env.OLLAMA_HOST, http://127.0.0.1:11434, http://localhost:11434] and caches the first reachable base (same pattern as providers.ts:409-411 and Faz10 host-bridge bridgeFetch). Use it in ai.ts listModels/generate/stream. Same .env then works in docker AND local.
findings.envDependent: broken when OLLAMA_HOST=docker value + running outside docker; works inside docker — but still a real graceful-degradation/consistency bug vs providers.ts
